body.title: Reconcile reading_worker_pool [PLACEHOLDER] vs IGOR_READING_EXTRACT=true
body.result: Updated theigors/subsystem_index/reading_worker_pool with 'DISTINCT FROM' note pointing at the live reading subsystem; [PLACEHOLDER] tag preserved (worker pool not built).
body.description:
Palace node theigors/subsystem_index/reading_worker_pool was tagged [PLACEHOLDER] but IGOR_READING_EXTRACT=true is on, creating reader confusion: 'is reading shipping or not?' Both are true — the live reading subsystem (reading_engine.py + chunker.py + reading_indexer.py + ebook_reader.py) IS shipping; the worker_pool is a future parallelization layer that does NOT exist.

DONE 2026-04-27: Updated theigors/subsystem_index/reading_worker_pool content with explicit 'DISTINCT FROM theigors/subsystem_index/reading' note + when-confused decision tree. [PLACEHOLDER] tag preserved (correct — pool not built).

Affected files:
- clan.memory_palace path='theigors/subsystem_index/reading_worker_pool' (UPDATE).

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: the live reading node already points at reading_engine.py docstring; the placeholder node points at it via 'distinct from' link.
- postgres-only: ✓.
- test-plan-or-why-not: verification IS reading the SELECT output to confirm cross-link.

Scope boundary:
IN: reading_worker_pool node content.
OUT: building the actual worker pool (separate L/XL ticket when sprint starts), refactoring reading_engine.py.

Test plan:
- SELECT content FROM clan.memory_palace WHERE path='theigors/subsystem_index/reading_worker_pool' contains 'DISTINCT FROM' and points to theigors/subsystem_index/reading.
- Verified 2026-04-27.
